title: Microsoft IIS Application Pool Recycling Events
dataSources: `index=windows` IIS configuration / Application operational logs, `WinEventLog:Application` W3SVC-WP recycle messages
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (`Splunk_TA_windows`); Splunk Add-on for Microsoft IIS (Splunkbase 3185)
spl:
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-IIS-Configuration/Operational" OR sourcetype="WinEventLog:Application"
| where EventCode IN (5074,5075,5117,5118,5119) OR match(_raw, "(?i)application pool.*recycle")
| stats count by EventCode, host, Message
| sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Recycling resets in-process state and can explain user session loss or cold-start latency spikes. Distinguishing scheduled, idle-timeout, and error-driven recycles focuses remediation on configuration versus code defects.
value: Cuts mean time to innocence for application teams during IIS instability windows.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for signs that cuts mean time to innocence for application teams during IIS instability windows.
implementation: Install Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (`Splunk_TA_windows`); enable IIS operational channels. Correlate EventCode meanings with MSDN. Tag planned maintenance.
visualization: Stacked bars for status/substatus, Perfmon timecharts, top client tables.
knownFalsePositives: Spikes or gaps can follow approved maintenance, load tests, or short collection outages. We add a second signal and a change window before escalating.
